Documentation: Subsidy Variable Construction
Main Sheet
The Main Sheet contains the raw data and intermediate calculations for the subsidy variable. The columns are:
Column A — Months: Date in YYYY-MM format, from 2005-01 to 2022-12.
Column B — Price_raw: Domestic retail gasoline price in Iranian Rials per liter. Source: Ross et al. (2017) replication data via Von Uexküll et al. (2024) for 2005–2015; Trading Economics for 2016–2022.
Column C — unit_raw: Unit of the raw price (rial_per_liter or usd_per_liter).
Column D — source: Data source — ross/iran_rial_series for 2005–2015, trading_economics for 2016–2022.
Column E — price_usd_per_liter: Domestic price in nominal USD per liter. For Rial prices: Price_raw ÷ fx_rial_per_usd. For USD prices: taken directly.
Column F — fx_rial_per_usd: Exchange rate Rials per USD. Source: BIS end-of-period IRR/USD rates. Validated against IMF International Financial Statistics — difference is minimal (~1 Rial for January 2005), confirming that the exchange rate source does not drive any discrepancies.
Column G — notes: Methodological notes on the calculation.
Column H — benchmark_price_usd_per_gallon: New York Harbor (NYH) Conventional Gasoline Regular Spot Price FOB in USD per gallon. Source: US Energy Information Administration (EIA), series eer_epmru_pf4_y35ny, monthly frequency. This is the international reference price used by Ross et al. (2017).
Column I — benchmark_usd_per_liter_ross: Benchmark price in nominal USD per liter following the Ross method: (H ÷ 3.785) + 0.10. The factor 3.785 converts gallons to liters. The $0.10 represents fixed distribution costs as estimated by Coady et al. (2010), cited in both Ross et al. (2017) and Von Uexküll et al. (2024). Note that the distribution cost adjustment raises the benchmark — meaning the benchmark is the international spot price plus the cost of getting fuel to consumers, which represents the true supply cost that a government must cover.
Column J — cpi: US Consumer Price Index (All Urban Consumers, CPIAUCSL), monthly. Source: Federal Reserve Economic Database (FRED). The 2015 annual average is 237.002, which serves as the base year.
Column K — deflator: 237.002 ÷ CPI_month. Multiplying nominal USD prices by this deflator converts them to constant 2015 USD, making prices comparable across time.
Column L — price_usd_2015: Domestic price in constant 2015 USD: price_usd_per_liter × deflator.
Column M — benchmark_usd_2015: Benchmark price in constant 2015 USD: benchmark_usd_per_liter_ross × deflator.
Column N — bmgap2015adj: Absolute subsidy in constant 2015 USD per liter: price_usd_2015 − benchmark_usd_2015. Negative values indicate a subsidy (domestic price below benchmark); positive values indicate an implicit tax. Note: In March–May 2020, values are slightly positive due to the COVID-19 oil price crash, which briefly pushed the international benchmark below the Iranian domestic price. This is substantively correct and not a data error.

Documentation: Subsidy Variable Construction
subsidy_final Sheet

The subsidy_final sheet contains the final subsidy variable and all quantities needed for validation. The columns are:

Column A — date: Month of observation in YYYY-MM format, from 2005-01 to 2022-12.
Column B — price_usd_2015: Domestic gasoline price in Iran in constant 2015 USD per liter, deflated from the raw price series using the US CPI.
Column C — benchmark_2015_adj: World market reference price in constant 2015 USD per liter. Calculated as the NYH conventional gasoline spot price divided by 3.785 (liters per gallon) plus $0.10 per liter for distribution costs, deflated by the US CPI. Follows the method of Ross et al. (2017) as applied in Von Uexküll et al. (2024).
Column D — bmgap2015adj: Subsidy gap in constant 2015 USD per liter, defined as the domestic price minus the benchmark price. Negative values indicate a subsidy. For 2005–2015, values are taken directly from Von Uexküll et al. (2024); for 2015–2022, values are calculated using the same method with EIA NYH spot price data and FRED CPIAUCSL.
Column E — price_growth_3m: Three-month rolling growth rate of the domestic price. Used to identify subsidy reform episodes characterized by sharp price increases.
Column F — bmgap_own: Own benchmark calculation for the full 2005–2022 period using the Ross/Von Uexküll method throughout. Calculated independently of Column D and used for validation purposes.
Column G — abs_diff: Absolute difference between bmgap2015adj (Column D) and bmgap_own (Column F), sign-corrected. Serves as the primary validation metric, measuring the divergence between the own calculation and the Von Uexküll series over the 2005–2015 overlap period.
